idea-plugin.name: Dependency Screamer
idea-plugin.description:
Dependency Screamer

Checks your pom.xml Maven dependencies against a Nexus Repository and warns when newer versions are available.

Key Features

  • Nexus integration — queries Nexus Repository REST API with full pagination support
  • Group filter — only scan dependencies matching configurable group prefixes (e.g. com.endava)
  • Tool Window — beautiful card-based UI with animated spinner, summary badges, and staggered reveals
  • Clickable Nexus links — outdated dependencies link directly to the artifact in Nexus browser
  • Code annotations — gutter icons and warnings on outdated <version> tags
  • Quick-fix — one-click update to the latest version
  • Property resolution — handles ${jackson.version} style Maven properties
  • Secure credentials — passwords stored via IntelliJ PasswordSafe
  • Fully async — no UI blocking

Getting Started

  1. Go to Settings → Tools → Dependency Screamer
  2. Enter your Nexus URL, repository name(s), and optional credentials
  3. Open the Dependency Screamer tool window and click Scan
idea-plugin.change-notes:
1.0.0

  • Initial release
  • Maven POM dependency scanning with property resolution
  • Nexus Repository integration with pagination and authentication
  • Configurable group filter for targeted scanning
  • Tool Window with animated UI, summary badges, and card-based results
  • Clickable Nexus links for outdated dependencies
  • XML inspection, gutter annotations, and quick-fix actions
  • Setup wizard for first-time configuration
